label: DNS zone transfer
recommendation: Restrict TCP/53; zone transfers often should not cross untrusted boundaries.

label: TFTP
recommendation: Do not expose TFTP to untrusted networks; prefer SCP/SFTP or signed distribution channels.

label: Link (legacy)
recommendation: Retire or isolate legacy Link protocol; restrict to tightly controlled admin segments if unavoidable.

label: SUN RPC portmapper
recommendation: Restrict RPC portmapper to internal segments; block from internet-facing perimeters.

label: BSD r-commands (rexec/rlogin/rshell)
recommendation: Disable r-commands; use SSH with strong authentication instead.

label: LPD (line printer)
recommendation: Limit LPD to print servers inside trusted zones; avoid exposure beyond internal VLANs.

label: UUCP
recommendation: Treat UUCP as legacy; remove from perimeter or restrict to known batch partners.

label: OpenWindows
recommendation: Block legacy OpenWindows; use modern remote display controls with encryption.

label: NFS
recommendation: Keep NFS on private networks; use Kerberos exports and firewall rules; never expose raw NFS to the internet.

label: X11 display
recommendation: Do not forward X11 across untrusted paths; use VPN or SSH -X with caution and disable when unused.

label: Small services (ports 1-20)
recommendation: Broad low ports; tune list for your environment.

label: FTP
recommendation: Prefer SFTP/HTTPS; if FTP is required, use explicit FTPS and restrict source IPs.

label: SSH
recommendation: Common admin port; ensure posture matches policy when exposed.

label: Telnet
recommendation: Replace Telnet with SSH; cleartext remote login should not cross untrusted boundaries.

label: SMTP
recommendation: Limit inbound SMTP to dedicated mail relays; monitor for open relay misconfiguration.

label: Finger
recommendation: Disable Finger; it discloses user information useful to attackers.

label: POP2/POP3
recommendation: Prefer IMAPS/POP3S on edge; enforce TLS and authentication for mail retrieval.

label: NNTP
recommendation: Restrict NNTP to internal news servers if still needed; uncommon on modern perimeters.

label: NTP
recommendation: Point clients to internal NTP servers; rate-limit and monitor UDP/123 for amplification abuse.

label: NetBIOS (RPC)
recommendation: Block NetBIOS from the internet; contain within Windows management segments.

label: NetBIOS name/datagram
recommendation: Same as other NetBIOS: internal-only; never from untrusted WAN.

label: NetBIOS session
recommendation: Use SMB over QUIC or VPN for remote access instead of exposing 139 directly.

label: IMAP
recommendation: Publish IMAPS (993) at the edge when needed; restrict plain IMAP to internal networks.

label: SNMP
recommendation: Use SNMPv3, strong community strings or auth, and limit agents to monitoring subnets only.

label: BGP
recommendation: BGP belongs on routers and peering links only; never expose to general internet hosts.

label: LDAP
recommendation: Prefer LDAPS (636) at boundaries; bind LDAP 389 to internal directory replicas only.

label: HTTPS
recommendation: Often legitimate; flagged as informational only.

label: SMB (Windows file sharing)
recommendation: Block SMB from the internet; require VPN or Zero Trust broker for remote file access.

label: Syslog
recommendation: Send syslog to collectors over dedicated management networks; validate sender ACLs.

label: SOCKS proxy
recommendation: Authenticate SOCKS; restrict who may use it to avoid becoming an open relay.

label: Alternate HTTP
recommendation: Non-standard HTTP on 8000; allow only for known apps, document owners, and monitor for abuse.

label: Alternate HTTP
recommendation: Non-standard HTTP on 8080; allow only for known apps, document owners, and monitor for abuse.

label: Alternate HTTP
recommendation: Non-standard HTTP on 8888; allow only for known apps, document owners, and monitor for abuse.

label: Cisco AUX-style
recommendation: Restrict device management ports to jump hosts and out-of-band management VLANs.

label: Cisco AUX stream-style
recommendation: Same as other Cisco aux-style ports: management plane only, not internet-facing.

label: Cisco AUX binary-style
recommendation: Same as other Cisco aux-style ports: management plane only, not internet-facing.

label: NFS lockd / nlockmgr
recommendation: Keep lockd with NFS on internal networks; pair with same controls as NFS 2049.

label: RDP
recommendation: Prefer VPN or gateway broker for RDP; enforce NLA, MFA, and IP allow lists if exposed.

label: VNC
recommendation: Tunnel VNC through SSH or VPN; do not expose cleartext VNC to untrusted networks.

label: Microsoft SQL Server
recommendation: Never publish SQL Server directly to the internet; use app tier and private connectivity.

label: MySQL / MariaDB
recommendation: Bind to private interfaces; require TLS and least-privilege DB accounts from app tier only.

label: PostgreSQL
recommendation: Same as other databases: internal networks, connection pooling, no public listeners.

label: Redis
recommendation: Redis must not be internet-facing; require AUTH/TLS and bind to loopback or private VLANs.

label: MongoDB
recommendation: Enable authentication and encryption; restrict to application subnets behind API gateways.

label: Elasticsearch HTTP
recommendation: Protect with auth, TLS, and network ACLs; Elasticsearch has a history of exposure incidents.

label: WinRM HTTP/HTTPS
recommendation: Limit WinRM to management VLANs; prefer HTTPS (5986) and harden with JEA where possible.
